description: A square designed graphic in ICE's 'THE WORST FIRST' rogues-gallery template (this version without the small red corner banner, with a large red diagonal 'HOUSTON' banner across the bottom-right corner instead). The background is the standard desaturated, high-contrast black-and-white macro photograph of handcuffed human hands against a dark body, used generically since the subject is identified only by nationality. The top-left headline is light cyan bold sans-serif 'ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL,' with white sub-line '1/26/2025 | Houston, TX.' In place of a name, large white bold sans-serif reads 'Mexican National.' Below is a 'CRIMES:' label with bulleted charges 'Aggravated assault with a deadly weapon' and 'Possession of a controlled substance.' The DHS seal and white 'ICE' wordmark sit at bottom left. The fixed agency template pairs an anonymized handcuffs image with a nationality label and emphasized violent/drug charges as a propaganda device casting immigrant detainees as dangerous criminals to justify removals. Audience: the general public and immigration-enforcement supporters.
notable_text: ARRESTED AND PENDING REMOVAL; 1/26/2025 | Houston, TX; Mexican National; CRIMES: Aggravated assault with a deadly weapon, Possession of a controlled substance; HOUSTON; ICE
